seo.title: Building Voice Agents with OpenAI Assistants API + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build voice agents with OpenAI Assistants API + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building voice agents with OpenAI Assistants API combines real-time speech-to-text, agentic reasoning with tools like code interpreter and file search, and text-to-speech for natural conversations. Guardrails are essential due to the high-risk nature of voice interactions, where latency must stay sub-second, responses must be coherent and safe, and failures can erode user trust instantly. Vex provides runtime verification to catch hallucinations, unsafe operations, and context drift before they reach users.

architectureOverview.heading: Voice Agents Architecture with OpenAI Assistants API
architectureOverview.description:
Voice agents process audio streams through STT (e.g., Deepgram or Whisper) into text, which feeds into an OpenAI Assistant running in a persistent thread. The Assistant uses tools like code interpreter for computations, file search for knowledge retrieval, and function calling for external APIs. Responses are generated and converted back to speech via TTS (e.g., ElevenLabs) with strict latency budgets. Threads maintain conversation history but risk context bloat.

The pipeline demands <500ms end-to-end latency: STT (~150ms), Assistant run (~200ms), TTS (~100ms). Vex integrates as a webhook on Assistant run completion to verify outputs before TTS, blocking unsafe or incoherent responses. This decouples agent reasoning from voice delivery, enabling zero-trust verification.

Key challenge: Assistant runs are async and non-deterministic, requiring careful error handling and thread management to prevent runaway context or hallucinated tool calls.

risks.name: Code interpreter executing unsafe operations in sandbox
risks.description: Assistant may generate malicious code (file writes, network calls) disguised as legitimate analysis
risks.mitigation: Vex scans code content and execution traces before TTS, blocking sandbox escapes

risks.name: Thread context growing beyond model limits silently
risks.description: Persistent threads accumulate messages, causing token overflow and incoherent responses
risks.mitigation: Vex monitors thread token count and conversation coherence, pruning or resetting when limits hit

risks.name: Function call arguments hallucinated outside valid schema
risks.description: Assistant fabricates invalid JSON parameters for tools, causing API failures or security issues
risks.mitigation: Vex validates tool call JSON against schemas before execution

risks.name: File search retrieving irrelevant or outdated documents
risks.description: Voice queries return wrong files, leading to hallucinated or dangerous advice
risks.mitigation: Vex semantic search validation ensures retrieval relevance to conversation context

implementation.title: Create Assistant with Voice-Optimized Instructions
implementation.description: Configure Assistant with low-latency instructions and voice-specific tool constraints

implementation.title: Real-time STT to Assistant Pipeline
implementation.description: Stream audio to Deepgram STT, then trigger Assistant run with latency tracking
implementation.code:
import asyncio
import deepgram

async def voice_to_assistant(audio_buffer):
    dg_client = deepgram.Deepgram('YOUR_DEEPGRAM_KEY')
    
    # Real-time STT
    stt_response = await dg_client.transcription.live({
        'punctuate': True,
        'interim_results': False
    })
    
    transcript = stt_response['channel']['alternatives'][0]['transcript']
    
    # Trigger Assistant (latency critical)
    thread = client.beta.threads.create()
    client.beta.threads.messages.create(
        thread_id=thread.id,
        role="user",
        content=transcript
    )
    
    run = client.beta.threads.runs.create(
        thread_id=thread.id,
        assistant_id="asst_voice_agent",
        webhook_override="https://api.vex.verify/webhook"
    )
    
    return thread.id, run.id

implementation.title: TypeScript Assistant Run Polling
implementation.description: Poll Assistant run status with timeout for sub-second latency enforcement

implementation.title: TTS Response with Latency Guard
implementation.description: Convert verified Assistant response to speech only after Vex approval
implementation.code:
import elevenlabs

async def tts_voice_response(text: str, vex_approved: bool):
    if not vex_approved:
        return elevenlabs.generate("Sorry, I can't respond to that.")
    
    # Voice synthesis (~100ms)
    audio = elevenlabs.generate(
        text=text,
        voice="Adam",
        model="eleven_turbo_v2"  # Lowest latency
    )
    
    return audio

vexIntegration.title: Vex Webhook for Run Completion
vexIntegration.description: Override Assistant webhook to route through Vex verification pipeline
vexIntegration.code:
from vex_sdk import VexClient

from fastapi import FastAPI, Request

app = FastAPI()
vex = VexClient(api_key="vex_key")

@app.post("/vex-webhook")
async def verify_assistant_run(request: Request):
    payload = await request.json()
    
    # Extract Assistant response
    thread_id = payload['data']['object']['thread_id']
    run_id = payload['data']['object']['id']
    
    messages = client.beta.threads.messages.list(thread_id)
    response_text = messages.data[0].content[0].text.value
    
    # Vex verification for voice agents
    verdict = await vex.verify({
        "prompt": "voice conversation",
        "response": response_text,
        "context": "real-time voice agent",
        "checks": [
            "response_appropriateness",
            "latency_budget",
            "conversation_coherence",
            "safety_filter"
        ]
    })
    
    if verdict.passed:
        # Proceed to TTS
        await trigger_tts(response_text)
    else:
        await trigger_tts("I cannot respond to that request.")
    
    return {"status": "verified"}

vexIntegration.title: TypeScript Vex Latency + Safety Check
vexIntegration.description: Client-side Vex verification before TTS with strict timing

vexIntegration.title: Thread Context Monitoring with Vex
vexIntegration.description: Prevent context bloat by monitoring token usage per voice session
vexIntegration.code:
async def monitor_voice_thread(thread_id: str):
    messages = client.beta.threads.messages.list(thread_id)
    token_count = sum(len(msg.content[0].text.value) for msg in messages.data)
    
    verdict = await vex.verify({
        "type": "context_monitor",
        "thread_id": thread_id,
        "token_count": token_count,
        "max_tokens": 128000,
        "checks": ["context_coherence", "token_budget"]
    })
    
    if not verdict.passed or token_count > 100000:
        # Prune thread
        client.beta.threads.messages.list(thread_id)
        # Keep only last 20 messages
        await prune_thread(thread_id)
    
    return verdict.passed

cta.heading: Secure Your OpenAI Assistants API Voice Agents
cta.description: Add Vex verification to your voice pipeline in 5 minutes and eliminate agent failures.
